measurement_note: No GPU measurements were added with this index. Existing online-attention-forward seed data is timing-only and lacks FlashAttention, MIOpen, Triton/framework, vLLM on ROCm, vLLM, FlashInfer, and materialized GEMM baselines.
library_stance: Libraries are competitors to beat or match.
Libraries are correctness oracles for matching contracts.
Libraries are profiling targets and implementation references.
Libraries are extension surfaces for plugins, graph integration, generated kernels, or serving-stack replacement.
Libraries are not final answers; when a library wins, record why and define the next narrower custom-kernel hypothesis.

seed_contract.formula: O = softmax(scale * Q * K^T + mask) * V
seed_contract.query_position_convention: suffix-aligned causal positions: absolute_query = query_index + max(0, key_length - query_length); key is visible when key_index <= absolute_query
seed_contract.decode_interpretation: query_length=1,key_length=N,causal=1 models latest-token decode and can see the whole KV cache

overall_ladder.notes: Use double-precision stable softmax with the exact mask and suffix-aligned query convention.
Useful oracle and memory-traffic floor; usually weak for long-sequence fused prefill.
Exploit fixed shape, layout, mask, page size, fusion, lower launch overhead, or deployment boundary.

baseline_ladders.name: FlashAttention dense and KV-cache paths
ladder: Use flash_attn_func for separate Q, K, V tensors.
Use flash_attn_qkvpacked_func only if QKV is already packed or packing is timed.
Use flash_attn_varlen_func when padding or ragged sequence lengths are part of the contract.
Use flash_attn_with_kvcache for decode or extend with KV-cache update and attention in the same path.
Use architecture-specific FlashAttention variants only when the GPU, dtype, and head dimension are supported.
must_match: causal bottom-right or suffix-aligned mask when query_length != key_length
layout conversion from [B,H,S,D] to [B,S,H,D] is included unless upstream tensors already use the library layout
dtype and accumulator contract are recorded; FP16/BF16/FP8 baselines are not equivalent to the FP32 seed unless declared as a changed numeric contract
ALiBi, local window, rotary, soft cap, and attention sinks are disabled unless the custom kernel implements them
timing_boundary: optional QKV pack, transpose, or cache update included only when required by the workload and stated
custom_followups: fixed-shape no-ragged/no-dropout kernel
fused scale, mask, rotary, bias, or output work
decode-only latency with fixed KV layout
plugin or extension path that avoids layout conversion

baseline_ladders.name: MIOpen scaled dot-product attention
applies_to: SDPA fprop expressible as a MIOpen graph
CDNA3 or CDNA4/RDNA4-oriented fused attention paths when supported
ladder: Build a MIOpen graph with matching Q, K, V, scale, mask, and output descriptors.
Generate operation plans and filter by dtype, compute type, workspace, determinism, dropout, mask behavior, and target GPU support.
Benchmark viable engine configs and record the selected engine, workspace bytes, and plan filters.
Compare steady-state graph execution against the custom seed with the same device-resident tensors.
If no equivalent plan exists, record baseline-unavailable instead of silently substituting a weaker baseline.
timing_boundary: plan creation and autotune measured separately
steady-state graph execution compared to kernel timings
layout conversion included when not a descriptor-only view
custom_followups: fixed-shape direct HIP where MIOpen has no equivalent engine
unsupported mask, bias, KV-cache, or output fusion
workspace-free or descriptor-specialized narrow kernel

baseline_ladders.name: Triton and framework attention
ladder: Run framework eager SDPA and record the selected backend.
Run framework compiled SDPA and separate compile time from steady-state runtime.
Run a Triton fused-attention kernel with block sizes, warps, stages, and persistence options recorded.
Optionally compare a CUDA graph captured framework or Triton path for repeated fixed shapes.
Move to HIP C++ after dispatch, JIT, and backend boundaries are clear.
must_match: dtype and TF32 allowance for FP32 framework paths
query-position convention for decode and extend
timing_boundary: first-call JIT and compile time reported separately
framework dispatch included for application-latency claims and excluded only for kernel-only claims
custom_followups: lower dispatch overhead for small fixed shapes
architecture-specific CUDA scheduling
fusion across framework op boundaries
MIGraphX plugin or C++ extension integration

baseline_ladders.name: vLLM on ROCm, vLLM, and FlashInfer decode or paged KV
ladder: Start with isolated contiguous decode matching query_length=1,key_length=N,causal=1 and latest-token query semantics.
Move to paged-KV decode with page or block size, block table, last-page length, KV layout, and cache dtype recorded.
Benchmark batched decode with realistic context-length distribution and scheduler assumptions.
Benchmark mixed prefill/decode or extend when the production path interleaves prompt processing and generation.
Use end-to-end serving metrics for serving claims: time-to-first-token, inter-token latency, tokens/sec, memory footprint, and correctness tolerance.
must_match: num query heads and num KV heads
KV layout and page or block size
rotary, ALiBI, sinks, sliding window, or other attention modifiers
scheduler and batch composition for serving metrics
timing_boundary: vLLM on ROCm engine build separated from runtime
FlashInfer plan separated from wrapper run unless replanning is runtime behavior
vLLM isolated attention benchmarks separated from serving benchmarks
KV-cache construction or layout conversion included when converting from seed tensors

migraphx_llm: max tokens in paged KV cache

custom_followups: fixed page size and head dimension
single-request or narrow-batch latency
custom cache layout
rotary or bias fusion
serving-stack kernel replacement with the same batching boundary

baseline_ladders.name: Materialized GEMM plus softmax plus GEMM
applies_to: small shapes where launch and materialization cost can be studied
ladder: Run batched or strided QK GEMM to materialize scores.
Apply scale, mask, and rowwise softmax with the seed suffix-aligned causal rule.
Run batched or strided PV GEMM to produce output.
Optionally capture the sequence in a CUDA graph for repeated fixed shapes.
Optionally run hipBLASLt algorithm search for QK and PV separately and record selected algorithms.
must_match: scale placement in GEMM alpha or softmax kernel
dtype of scores, probabilities, accumulators, and output
allocation and memory traffic for materialized scores and probabilities
launch count and graph replay boundary
timing_boundary: score/probability allocation if production would allocate it
custom_followups: online softmax to avoid materializing scores and probabilities
fused mask, softmax, and PV
fixed-shape row statistics in registers or shared memory
Matrix Core QK and PV tiles without materialized P

evidence_policy.timing_only_label: Use timing-only when results are HIP-event or framework timings without Nsight counters.
evidence_policy.negative_example_label: Use negative example when an attempted optimization or custom path does not improve speed.
must_not_claim: rocprofiler/rocprof counter evidence unless profiler artifacts were collected
FlashAttention/MIOpen/Triton equivalence unless mask, dtype, scale, layout, dropout, and sequence contract match
Decode wins unless KV-cache layout, batching, and query-position semantics are recorded
Serving wins from isolated attention-kernel timing alone
FP32 custom wins against lower-precision library baselines unless the changed numeric contract is explicit
